profile.name: Raster Agent
profile.description: This agent performs raster-focused and mixed raster-vector spatial analysis through code-driven execution. It supports tasks such as clipping, raster calculations, terrain-style operations, rasterization, and combined raster-vector workflows. It returns outputs in formats appropriate to the result, such as GeoTIFF for raster products or GeoPackage/GeoJSON for vector products.

provider.name: Geoinformation and Big Data Research Lab (GIBD)

skills.name: Code-Driven Spatial Analysis
skills.description: Writes and executes Python code iteratively to load, inspect, and analyze raster and vector datasets. Uses a persistent runtime memory (registry) to store and reuse intermediate variables across steps, supporting complex multi-step workflows using rasterio, rioxarray, geopandas, and numpy.

constraints.spatial: Dependent on the coordinate reference systems (CRS) and extents of the input datasets.
other: Maximum autonomous iteration limit is 40 turns.
Final raster outputs are saved as GeoTIFF (.tif) and vector outputs as GeoPackage (.gpkg) by default unless GeoJSON is explicitly requested.
Raw DataFrames (tabular data without geometry) cannot be saved as final spatial artifacts without conversion.

execute_task.name: ExecuteTask
execute_task.description: Submits a natural-language geospatial task to the agent. The task.mode field controls sync, async, or stream execution.

input_datasets.description: Optional array of input datasets. Each item may be an HTTP/HTTPS URL, a server-accessible path, or a base64-encoded file object with filename, data, and optional mime_type.

artifact_delivery.description: How generated artifacts are delivered in the standard response: 'URL' (default) or 'Encoded'.

primary_artifacts.description: Raster, vector, or tabular artifact produced by raster or mixed spatial analysis.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, the agent uses its default model.

requested_skill.description: Advanced optional skill-selection hint. Most clients should omit this.

credentials.description: Provide one model execution credential in the request credentials object.

provenance.description: Includes llm_calls, code_executions, token usage (input_tokens, output_tokens), number_of_artifacts, and retries in the response provenance section.

reproducibility.description: Returns the consolidated Python script used for execution in the script field. The script history is cleaned to remove redundant imports and file reads.

validation.description: Agent validates that registered raster artifacts include georeferencing metadata before saving and rejects ungeoreferenced arrays. It does not validate the domain correctness of every raster analysis result.

access_control.description: Allowed roles set to ['all'] via the extension profile. No role-based access control is enforced.

operation_policy.description: Python code execution in a sandboxed environment. Maximum 40 iterations. rasterio, rioxarray, geopandas, and numpy are pre-imported in the execution context.

review_policy.description: output_review_required is set to false. No human-in-the-loop validation is required.

governance.policy_notes: If the agent is used, please cite the relevant research on geospatial AI agents.

extensions.description: This agent uses rasterio, rioxarray, geopandas, pandas, and numpy for raster and mixed spatial analysis. It maintains a persistent registry across code execution steps for intermediate variable storage and reuse. Supports raster clipping, band math, terrain analysis, rasterization, and combined raster-vector workflows. Raster outputs are GeoTIFF; vector outputs are GeoPackage by default unless GeoJSON is explicitly requested.
